title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline (lint → build → test → docker)
description:
Set up multi-stage GitHub Actions CI pipeline in .github/workflows/ci.yml.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Stage 1: lint — runs eslint, fails fast
- [x] Stage 2: build — compiles TypeScript + Vite, uploads dist artifact
- [x] Stage 3: test — runs vitest unit tests (parallel with build)
- [x] Stage 4: docker — builds and pushes image to ghcr.io on push to main only
- [x] Pipeline triggers on push and pull_request to main
- [x] Docker stage depends on both build and test passing

title: E2E tests in CI pipeline
description:
Add a Playwright e2e stage to the GitHub Actions CI pipeline.

## Context
E2e tests exist in e2e/app.spec.ts (21 tests, Playwright + Chromium) but
are not currently run in CI. The pipeline only runs unit tests (npm test).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New e2e job added to ci.yml
- [ ] Job depends on build (reuses dist/ artifact) and test
- [ ] Playwright browsers installed via cache
- [ ] App served using `vite preview` via Playwright's webServer config
- [ ] Job runs on every push and PR to main
- [ ] Failure blocks the docker job
- [ ] playwright-report uploaded as artifact on failure for debugging

title: Code Quality Gates

close_reason: All children complete: ESLint security plugins (7zy.1) and 90% unit test coverage threshold (7zy.2).

title: ESLint security plugins (SAST)
description:
Integrate ESLint security plugins into the existing lint pipeline as a SAST gate.

## Plugins to add
- eslint-plugin-security — flags unsafe JS patterns (eval, regex DoS, prototype pollution, etc.)
- eslint-plugin-no-unsanitized — flags unsanitized DOM insertion (innerHTML, outerHTML, etc.)

## Changes
- Install both plugins as devDependencies
- Add both plugins and their recommended rule sets to eslint.config.js
- Verify `npm run lint` passes cleanly with new rules
- CI lint stage already runs `npm run lint` — no CI changes needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] eslint-plugin-security and eslint-plugin-no-unsanitized installed
- [ ] Both plugins active in eslint.config.js with recommended rules
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes with zero errors
- [ ] Any rule violations in existing code are either fixed or explicitly disabled with justification

title: Unit test coverage threshold at 90%
description:
Enforce 90% unit test coverage as a CI quality gate.

## Changes
- Narrow coverage include to src/ only; exclude main.tsx and ast.ts (pure type definitions)
- Add thresholds: statements 90, branches 90, functions 90, lines 90 in vitest.config.ts
- Add tests for App.tsx (currently 0%) and validator.ts (currently 71.66%) to meet threshold
- Update CI test stage to run `npm run test:coverage` and upload lcov artifact

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] vitest.config.ts has coverage include/exclude and thresholds configured
- [ ] `npm run test:coverage` passes locally with all thresholds met
- [ ] CI test stage runs coverage and fails build if threshold not met
- [ ] lcov.info uploaded as CI artifact
close_reason: 90% coverage thresholds enforced in vitest.config.ts (statements/branches/functions/lines). Final: stmts 95%, branches 93.6%, functions 98.5%, lines 95%. CI test stage runs test:coverage and uploads lcov artifact. Added 22 new unit tests: App.test.tsx (7), validator additions for CASE/FOR STEP/TypeDecl/file ops/array access (14), PseudocodeEditor error-line branch (1).

title: Reset output pane and editor when New Question is clicked
description:
When the student clicks New Question, the error/output panel retains the previous compilation result (e.g. 'No errors found.'). The editor code and compiled errors should both be cleared so the student starts fresh on the new question.

## Root Cause
App.tsx handleCompile sets errors state, but nextQuestion() only updates the question — it does not reset errors or code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clicking New Question clears the editor textarea
- [ ] Clicking New Question resets the output panel to its initial (blank/no-compile-yet) state
- [ ] Unit test: App — clicking New Question clears errors
- [ ] E2E test: clicking New Question after compiling resets the output panel

title: Compiler: error on empty code submission
description:
Clicking Compile with an empty editor shows 'No errors found.' Students should receive a clear message that there is nothing to compile.

## Root Cause
compile('') produces an empty AST. The validator finds no errors, so the output panel shows success.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Compiling empty or whitespace-only code shows a meaningful error message
- [ ] Unit test added
- [ ] E2E test added

title: Compiler: unknown type names accepted silently in DECLARE
description:
DECLARE n1 : I or DECLARE n1 : In compiles without errors because parseDataType() treats any IDENTIFIER as a user-defined NamedType, and the validator never checks whether that type is actually declared.

## Root Cause
validator.ts DeclareStatement handler defines the symbol but never calls checkDataType(). NamedType references are never resolved against the scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DECLARE n1 : I produces a 'Unknown type' error
- [ ] DECLARE n1 : INTEGER still compiles cleanly (PrimitiveType)
- [ ] DECLARE book : TBook compiles cleanly when TBook is declared via TYPE
- [ ] Unit tests added
- [ ] E2E test added

title: Epic: Pseudocode interpreter and solution evaluation
description:
The compiler currently validates syntax and semantics only. Students can write syntactically correct code that does not solve the question. Evaluating correctness requires:
- A pseudocode interpreter/runtime
- Per-question test cases (inputs + expected outputs)
- A 'Run & Evaluate' mode that executes the pseudocode against the test cases

This is a separate feature stream from compilation. Tracked here for future planning.

title: Tablet-responsive layout (iPad support)
description:
Add media queries and fix layout so all UI elements display correctly on tablets (768px+).

## Root causes
- .top fixed at 15vh with no flex wrapping — clips long question text
- .ask has white-space: nowrap causing truncation
- .errorPane fixed at 320px — too wide on 768px screens
- No media queries in any CSS module

## Changes
- App.module.css: add breakpoints at 768px; make top section auto-height with max constraint; stack bottom pane vertically on small tablets
- QuestionPanel.module.css: allow .ask to wrap; flex-wrap panel on narrow screens
- PseudocodeEditor.module.css: ensure toolbar and Compile button always visible
- ErrorPanel.module.css: reduce pane width on tablets

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All buttons (Compile, New Question) visible on iPad portrait (768x1024) and landscape (1024x768)
- [ ] Question text not truncated on tablet
- [ ] Error panel readable on tablet without squeezing editor
- [ ] No regression on desktop (1280px+)
- [ ] Unit tests updated

title: Rich question display with context panel
description:
Redesign QuestionPanel to handle multi-paragraph context like real Cambridge exam papers.

## Current problem
QuestionPanel shows title + ask in a single truncated line. Real questions have multi-paragraph context, module specs, tables, and code examples that need a scrollable display area.

## Changes
- Split top section into: title bar (badge + title + New Question button) and a scrollable context+ask area
- Context rendered as preformatted/markdown-style text with proper line breaks
- Ask section highlighted distinctly (e.g. purple border-left, bold)
- Top section height increases to ~30vh with overflow-y: auto
- QuestionPanel component and CSS updated
- QuestionPanel tests updated

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multi-paragraph context displays with line breaks
- [ ] Ask section visually distinct from context
- [ ] New Question button always visible in title bar
- [ ] Scrollable when content exceeds panel height
- [ ] Works on tablet and desktop

title: Expand question bank to 50 rich questions
description:
Rewrite all questions with multi-paragraph context matching real Cambridge AS exam paper style, and expand from 25 to 50 total.

## Changes
- Rewrite all 25 existing questions with rich context (scenario, module specs, examples)
- Add 25 new questions bringing total to 50
- Split: 20 simple, 20 intermediate, 10 complex
- Update generate-questions.ts
- Run npm run generate:questions to update questions.json
- Update schema tests: total count 25→50, split ratios updated

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 50 questions in questions.json (20 simple, 20 intermediate, 10 complex)
- [ ] Every question has a non-trivial multi-sentence context
- [ ] Every ask references specifics from the context
- [ ] All schema tests pass
- [ ] Questions representative of Cambridge AS level difficulty

title: Tablet-friendly layout: sticky New Question + full-width question + split bottom
description: Restructure layout to three rows: (1) New Question button full-width sticky, (2) question content full-width scrollable, (3) editor + errors side-by-side. Eliminates scroll-off issues for both buttons on tablet.
